headline: Open-weights coding models MiniMax M3 and GLM 5.2 anchored the day alongside an ensemble-routing release from OpenRouter.
body: Open-weights coding models drove the day's news: MiniMax M3 launched with million-token context and Anthropic and OpenAI endpoint compatibility plus Cursor and Cline integration, while GLM 5.2 was reviewed as a top open-source model spanning code generation, 3D modeling, and ray tracing [2][4]. Ensemble routing surfaced through OpenRouter's Fusion, which queries multiple models and synthesizes a unified output near a benchmark leader at roughly half the cost, tying into the kernel fusion and reliability movers [10]. On the agents front, NVIDIA released XR AI for building agents on AR and XR wearables, and an IBM Security panel debated whether agents should manage passwords amid a record Patch Tuesday [11][1]. Technique breakouts clustered around agent skills, scheduling, and software engineering.

cited_items.title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
